message.content:
You are verifier #1, independent of all other verifiers. Do NOT confirm findings optimistically — only confirm if you can independently verify the issue exists in the actual code at /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay.

Finding F-028: "MLflow run_id is None when start_run fails — all metrics silently dropped with no structured warning"
File: src/assay_engine/pipeline.py (line 324)
Description: If tracker.start_run() raises, run_id remains None. track() helper silently returns early. ALL metrics (n_supported, alignment_rate, etc.) silently dropped. No warning emitted.
PoC: Pass MlflowExperimentTracker pointing at non-existent SQLite path. Complete successful study. result.experiment_run_id is None and no metrics logged, no warning.

Instructions:
1. Read the actual file at /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py
2. Look at the code at line 324 and surrounding context
3. Independently verify whether the described problem EXISTS in the current code
4. Check if the PoC is reproducible from the actual code as written
5. Consider whether this is already fixed (pass-1 #101-#122, pass-2 #124-#156 fixed many issues)
6. Return your independent verdict

Confirmed = true ONLY if: (a) the code problem exists as described, (b) the PoC is reproducible from current code, (c) it is a genuine production-readiness concern. Set confirmed=false for speculative issues, issues already fixed, or issues where the PoC does not match what the code actually does.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

336	    try:
337	        # Bootstrap tracing once and correlate every phase/reasoning span to this run via baggage,
338	        # so a run's spans are findable by id and join the experiment-tracker run (#122).
339	        # Done INSIDE the try so a tracing-setup failure is still covered by the finally's

341	        # state (the #110 class of bug) if bootstrap/context entry raises (#156). Best-effort.
342	        bootstrap_tracing()
343	        corr_id = run_id or uuid.uuid4().hex
344	        trace_ctx.enter_context(run_trace_context(corr_id))
345	        # ---- INGEST ----
346	        with tracer.span("phase:INGEST"):
347	            enter(Phase.INGEST)
348	            try:
349	                corpus = defn.parser.parse(plan.source)
350	                source_fp = defn.parser.source_fingerprint(plan.source)
351	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — normalize adapter/IO ingestion failures
352	                raise IngestionError(f"parser failed to ingest {plan.source!r}: {exc}") from exc
353	            # The parser Protocol return type is unenforced at runtime; a misimplemented adapter
354	            # that returns None/list/dict would otherwise slip past the normalizer above and die
355	            # at `corpus.units` with an opaque AttributeError, violating the documented
356	            # "Raises IngestionError on a bad source" contract (#134).
357	            if not isinstance(corpus, Corpus):
358	                raise IngestionError(f"parser returned {type(corpus).__name__}, expected Corpus")
359	            if not isinstance(source_fp, str):
360	                raise IngestionError(
361	                    f"parser.source_fingerprint returned {type(source_fp).__name__}, expected str"
362	                )
363	            if not corpus.units:
364	                raise IngestionError("ingestion produced an empty corpus")
365	            cfp = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
366	            source_version: str | None = None
367	            if versioner is not None:
368	                source_version = versioner.put(str(plan.source))
369	            trail.record(
370	                "ingest",
371	                "parsed source into canonical corpus",
372	                source_fingerprint=source_fp,
373	                corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
374	                n_units=len(corpus.units),
375	                n_relations=len(corpus.relations),

377	            )

250	    gate_handler: GateHandler,
251	    tracer: Tracer | None = None,
252	    tracker: ExperimentTracker | None = None,
253	    versioner: DataVersioner | None = None,
254	    clock: Clock | None = None,
255	    secret: bytes | None = None,
256	    trail: ProvenanceTrail | None = None,
257	) -> StudyResult:
258	    """Run one study end-to-end through its governed phase sequence. See module docstring.
259	
260	    ``gate_handler`` is **required** (no default), so governance review is a conscious choice —
261	    pass :func:`auto_approve` to opt out of human review explicitly, or an operator handler that
262	    can block/park. ``tracer`` defaults to :class:`OtelTracer` (real spans if OpenTelemetry + a
263	    provider are present, a graceful no-op otherwise). ``tracker`` (optional) logs params/metrics
264	    to an experiment store, best-effort (a tracker failure never aborts the run). ``versioner``
265	    (optional) content-addresses the source so the trail cites retrievable bytes. ``clock``
266	    injects a deterministic clock for the provenance timestamps only (ordering checks always use
267	    real time). ``secret`` keys the internal provenance trail with HMAC (forgery-resistant);
268	    mutually exclusive with passing your own ``trail``. ``trail`` may be a caller-owned
269	    :class:`ProvenanceTrail` so a run that *raises* still leaves the caller the partial trail.
270	
271	    Raises ``IngestionError`` on a bad source, ``FirewallViolation``/``PreRegistrationError`` on
272	    a methodological breach, ``GateError`` if a gate blocks, and ``ValueError`` on a malformed run.
273	    """
274	    tracer = tracer or OtelTracer()
275	    if trail is not None and secret is not None:
276	        raise ValueError("pass either a caller-owned trail OR a secret to key a new one, not both")
277	    defn = plan.definition
278	    modes = defn.modes
279	    required = required_phases(modes)
280	    trail = trail if trail is not None else ProvenanceTrail(secret=secret, clock=clock)
281	    visited: list[Phase] = []
282	    run_id: str | None = None
283	
284	    def enter(phase: Phase, **payload: Any) -> None:
285	        visited.append(phase)
286	        trail.record("phase", f"enter {phase.name}", phase=phase.name, **payload)
287	
288	    def track(fn: Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]) -> None:
289	        # observability is best-effort: a tracker failure is recorded but never aborts a run.
290	        if tracker is None or run_id is None:
291	            return
292	        try:
293	            fn(tracker, run_id)
294	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — tracker is an external, optional backend
295	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker call failed: {exc}")
296

content.text:
The code is clear. Let me verify the full picture:

1. `run_id` is initialized to `None` at line 282.
2. If `tracker.start_run()` raises (line 324), the exception is caught (line 331), a `trail.record()` call is made (line 332) — but `run_id` remains `None`.
3. The `track()` helper at line 290 returns early when `run_id is None`, so all subsequent metric logging calls silently drop.
4. No `warnings.warn()` or `logging.warning()` is emitted — only a provenance trail internal record is made (not visible to calling code as a structured warning).

input.description: Check for any warnings emitted in pipeline.py

content.text: No `warnings` or `logging.warn` calls exist anywhere in the file. The finding is confirmed: when `start_run` raises, `run_id` stays `None`, all `track()` calls silently return early, and no structured warning is emitted to the caller — only an internal provenance trail entry (`trail.record("tracking_error", ...)`) which is not surfaced as a Python warning or log line.

input.reasoning:
The code at /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py confirms all three conditions:

1. run_id is initialized to None at line 282 and only assigned inside the try block at lines 324-330.
2. If tracker.start_run() raises, the except clause at line 331-332 catches it and records only a trail.record("tracking_error", ...) entry — run_id remains None.
3. The track() helper at lines 288-295 has an explicit guard: `if tracker is None or run_id is None: return` — so every subsequent metric log call (n_supported, alignment_rate, etc.) silently returns early.
4. A grep for warnings/logging.warn across the entire file returns zero results — no Python warnings module or logging.warning() call exists anywhere in pipeline.py. The provenance trail record is internal and not visible to calling code as a structured warning.

The PoC is reproducible: with a tracker whose start_run raises (e.g., non-existent SQLite path), the study completes successfully, result.experiment_run_id is None, all metrics are silently dropped, and no warning is emitted to the caller. This is a genuine production-readiness concern because silent metric loss with no observable signal makes it impossible to detect misconfigured trackers in production.
